steps.title: Substitute t = 0 into the function
steps.content:
The question asks for the temperature when the flask was placed on the table, which is the initial time $t = 0$. Substitute into the given function:
$$g(0) = 296 + (368 - 296)(2.72)^{-0.108(0)}$$

steps.title: Simplify and calculate
steps.content:
The exponent becomes $-0.108(0) = 0$. Since any nonzero number raised to the power 0 equals 1:
$$(2.72)^{0} = 1$$
Therefore:
$$g(0) = 296 + (368 - 296)(1) = 296 + 72 = 368$$
The temperature of the liquid when the flask was first placed on the table was approximately 368 kelvins.
